master_row.title: Quantum Information Units
master_row.canonical_statement: A quantum information unit (qunit) in the Trawinistic framework is defined as a minimal Hilbert space of dimension ddd associated with a quantum subsystem; its implementation may be qubit (d=2d=2d=2) or higher-dimensional, with states encoded in curvature-protected degrees of freedom.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an algebraic definition.
master_row.dictionary: A quantum information unit (qunit) in the Trawinistic framework is defined as a minimal Hilbert space of dimension (d) associated with a quantum subsystem; its implementation may be qubit ((d=2)) or higher‑dimensional, with states encoded in curvature‑protected degrees of freedom.
master_row.encyclopedia: Gauge symmetry breaking generates massive vector bosons. % CANONICAL PDF-ALIGNED VERSION Interpretive role: This entry is the doorway from abstract quantum theory into information processing. It supplies the unit from which qubits, higher-dimensional qudits, and channel constructions are built.
